9. Test case T-8: The HS can accept a connection release initiated on the AG.
10. Test case T-9: The AG can accept a connection release initiated on the HS with an in-band ring tone.

The tests also cover the ability for both parties to release the connection and the ability for the HS to restore the speaker volume settings after a connection release.

The following is a list of test IDs that cover the requirement for remote audio volume control: T-0, T-1, T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5, T-6, T-7, T-8.

The requirement states that the AG can control the gain of the microphone or speaker of the HS by sending unsolicited result codes +VGM or +VGS respectively. The requirement also states that support for remote audio volume control is optional, and both the peaker and microphone gain are represented as parameters to the +VGM and +VGS, on a scale from 0 to 15. The values are absolute values, relating to a particular (implementation-dependent) volume level controlled by the HS. The HS may store the VGS and VGM settings at connection release. At connection establishment, the HS may restore the previous settings using the AT+VGS and AT+VGM commands. In case physical mechanisms (buttons, dials etc.) means are implemented on the HS to control the volume levels, the HS should also use the AT+VGS and AT+VGM commands to inform the AG of any changes in the volume levels.

The following test IDs cover the requirement for remote audio volume control: T-0, T-1, T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5, T-6, T-7, T-8.

The tests "T-0" and "T-1" cover the requirement for the AG to establish a complete audio connection towards the HS and for the HS to accept the connection.

The tests "T-2" and "T-3" cover the requirement for the AG to cause the HS to give an in-band ringing tone during connection establishment initiated from the AG and for the HS to give an in-band ringing tone upon user action on the HS.

The tests "T-4" and "T-5" cover the requirement for the AG to accept a connection release initiated from the HS and for the HS to accept a connection release initiated from the AG.

The tests "T-6" and "T-7" cover the requirement for the AG to provide an in-band ring tone and for the HS to give an in-band ringing tone upon user action on the HS during connection establishment initiated from the AG.

The tests "T-8" and "T-9" cover the requirement for the AG to accept a connection release initiated from the HS and for the HS to accept an audio connection transfer from the AG to the HS initiated by a user action on the HS or internal event on the AG.

The tests "T-10" and "T-11" cover the requirement for the AG to accept a connection release initiated from the HS and for the HS to accept an audio connection transfer from the AG to the HS initiated by a user action on the AG or internal event on the HS.

The tests "T-12" and "T-13" cover the requirement for the AG to accept a connection release initiated from the HS and for the HS to accept an audio connection transfer from the AG to the HS initiated by a user action on the HS or internal event on the AG, and for the HS to restore the speaker volume settings to the previous settings upon establishing a new connection.

The tests "T-14" and "T-15" cover the requirement for the AG to accept a connection release initiated from the HS and for the HS to restore the speaker volume settings to the previous settings upon establishing a new connection.

I hope this helps clarify the test IDs that cover the requirement for remote audio volume control. Let me know if you have any questions or need further clarification!
